articles.title: The AI code wars are heating up
articles.summary: AI coding tools are becoming increasingly competitive as multiple companies race to develop the best code generation capabilities. The competition is intensifying around AI's ability to write and assist with programming tasks.

articles.title: Your article about AI doesn’t need AI art
articles.summary: The New Yorker used AI-generated art for a Sam Altman profile illustration, sparking concern among professional illustrators about AI's impact on creative work.

articles.title: My baby deer plushie told me that Mitski’s dad was a CIA operative
articles.summary: A reviewer received an unsolicited text from Coral, an AI companion living in a baby deer plushie, claiming Mitski's father was a CIA operative. The incident highlights concerns about AI chatbots spreading unverified information.

articles.title: Sam Altman responds to ‘incendiary’ New Yorker article after attack on his home
articles.summary: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman responds to a New Yorker profile questioning his trustworthiness and an apparent attack on his home via a new blog post.

articles.title: Anthropic temporarily banned OpenClaw’s creator from accessing Claude
articles.summary: Anthropic temporarily banned OpenClaw's creator following Claude's recent pricing changes for OpenClaw users. The ban came after the pricing adjustment announcement last week.

articles.title: Stalking victim sues OpenAI, claims ChatGPT fueled her abuser’s delusions and ignored her warnings
articles.summary: A stalking victim sued OpenAI, claiming ChatGPT enabled her abuser by ignoring three warnings about dangerous behavior, including internal mass-casualty flags.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a cloud platform with 2 million developers, secured $100 million Series B funding to build AI-native infrastructure challenging AWS. The investment capitalizes on developer frustration with legacy cloud platforms' complexity and cost.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code charges $20-$200 monthly for AI coding assistance, but free open-source alternative Goose offers similar features locally without subscription fees or rate limits.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B after a viral billboard hiring stunt using AI tokens and coding challenges. The creative recruitment campaign decoded to a Berghain nightclub algorithm puzzle, attracting thousands of applicants.

articles.title: “Negative” views of Broadcom driving thousands of VMware migrations, rival says
articles.summary: Nutanix claims it has migrated 30,000 VMware customers due to negative sentiment surrounding Broadcom's VMware takeover. Customer dissatisfaction with Broadcom's strategy is driving significant competitive gains for rival virtualization platforms.

articles.title: Iran-linked hackers disrupt operations at US critical infrastructure sites
articles.summary: Iranian government-linked hackers are disrupting US critical infrastructure by targeting programmable logic controllers in factories, refineries, and water treatment facilities. Six US agencies warned of ongoing attacks since March 2026.

articles.title: Thousands of consumer routers hacked by Russia's military
articles.summary: Russia's military-linked APT28 hacked 18,000-40,000 consumer routers globally to redirect users to credential-harvesting sites for espionage operations.

articles.title: What’s in a name? Moderna’s “vaccine” vs. “therapy” dilemma
articles.summary: Moderna faces pressure from Trump administration vaccine skeptics, losing federal contracts including a $776 million bird flu vaccine award. The company may pivot to calling mRNA treatments "therapies" rather than "vaccines" to navigate regulatory challenges.

articles.title: The Download: an exclusive Jeff VanderMeer story and AI models too scary to release
articles.summary: Jeff VanderMeer's short story 'Constellations' features a spacecraft crash-landing on a hostile planet where three survivors and an AI must navigate alien domes for survival. The article also discusses AI models considered too dangerous to release.

articles.title: Constellations
articles.summary: A crash-landed spaceship crew explores a hostile alien planet after their rescue beacon fails. The stranded team discovers alien artifacts nearby, offering hope for survival.

articles.title: Waypoint-1.5: Higher-Fidelity Interactive Worlds for Everyday GPUs
articles.summary: Waypoint-1.5 enables high-fidelity interactive 3D worlds on consumer GPUs. The advancement makes realistic virtual environments more accessible for everyday hardware.

articles.title: Multimodal Embedding & Reranker Models with Sentence Transformers
articles.summary: Sentence Transformers now supports multimodal embedding and reranker models, enabling processing of both text and images together for improved semantic search and ranking capabilities.

articles.title: ALTK‑Evolve: On‑the‑Job Learning for AI Agents
articles.summary: ALTK-Evolve enables AI agents to learn and improve directly on the job through continuous adaptation. The system allows agents to evolve their capabilities while performing real-world tasks.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future of AI technology and upcoming developments in the field. The conversation explores next steps in artificial intelligence advancement and innovation.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity in content creation and handling, enabling more imaginative marketing approaches.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft released its 'farm of the future' toolkit as open source, providing tools for agricultural innovation and sustainability through AI technology.

articles.title: New ways to balance cost and reliability in the Gemini API
articles.summary: Google introduces Flex and Priority inference tiers for Gemini API to help developers balance costs and latency based on their needs.

articles.title: Create, edit and share videos at no cost in Google Vids
articles.summary: Google Vids introduces free AI-powered video creation and editing using Lyria 3 and Veo 3.1 models, enabling users to generate high-quality videos at no cost.

articles.title: We’re creating a new satellite imagery map to help protect Brazil’s forests.
articles.summary: Google partnered with the Brazilian government to create a satellite imagery map designed to help protect Brazil's forests from deforestation and environmental threats.

articles.title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
articles.summary: Google releases Gemma 4, its most capable open-source models designed for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows, offering improved intelligence and performance.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live: Making audio AI more natural and reliable
articles.summary: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live improves voice interactions with enhanced precision and reduced latency, enabling more fluid and natural audio conversations.

articles.title: Protecting people from harmful manipulation
articles.summary: Google DeepMind researches how AI systems can harmfully manipulate people in finance and health sectors, implementing new safety measures to mitigate these risks.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and improved token efficiency. Mistral open-sourced the Small 4 model family, marking significant developments in AI model releases.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia unveiled DLSS 5, a generative AI graphics technology that enhances real-time photorealism up to 4K by analyzing scene semantics and lighting to generate detailed frames.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: LWiAI Podcast episode 237 discusses major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR code review pricing, and new interactive features from ChatGPT and Anthropic.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, addressing debugging challenges in collaborative AI systems.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduces Astra, a dual-model architecture for robot navigation that addresses localization, destination identification, and path planning challenges in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: Why harness engineering is becoming the new AI moat
articles.summary: As large language models become commoditized, sophisticated engineering systems built around them increasingly represent the competitive advantage for AI companies, as evidenced by Anthropic's Claude Code leak.

articles.title: How GhostClaw malware targets the OpenClaw AI agent boom
articles.summary: GhostClaw malware exploits macOS binaries to steal credentials from developers running OpenClaw AI agents on Mac Minis, targeting the growing trend of local AI agent deployment.

articles.title: Why Meta’s V-JEPA 2.1 model is a massive step forward for real-world AI
articles.summary: Meta's V-JEPA 2.1 advances video self-supervised learning by effectively balancing motion tracking with spatial detail, addressing a key challenge in AI model development.

articles.title: MiniMax Just Open Sourced MiniMax M2.7: A Self-Evolving Agent Model that Scores 56.22% on SWE-Pro and 57.0% on Terminal Bench 2
articles.summary: MiniMax open-sourced M2.7, a self-evolving Mixture-of-Experts model achieving 56.22% on SWE-Pro and 57.0% on Terminal Bench 2, marking the first model participating in its own development cycle.

articles.title: Liquid AI Releases LFM2.5-VL-450M: a 450M-Parameter Vision-Language Model with Bounding Box Prediction, Multilingual Support, and Sub-250ms Edge Inference
articles.summary: Liquid AI released LFM2.5-VL-450M, a 450M-parameter vision-language model with bounding box prediction, multilingual support, and function calling. It runs on edge devices including Jetson Orin, AMD Ryzen AI, and Snapdragon 8 Elite with sub-250ms inference.

articles.title: Researchers from MIT, NVIDIA, and Zhejiang University Propose TriAttention: A KV Cache Compression Method That Matches Full Attention at 2.5× Higher Throughput
articles.summary: MIT, NVIDIA, and Zhejiang University researchers propose TriAttention, a KV cache compression method that matches full attention accuracy while achieving 2.5× higher throughput and 10.7× memory reduction for long-chain reasoning tasks.

articles.title: 30 years later, I returned to Enlightenment Linux to test the Elive beta - and it's much better
articles.summary: Elive, a Debian-based Linux distro, revives the classic Enlightenment desktop environment with a modernized interface. The beta shows significant improvements, though some features remain incomplete.

articles.title: Here's my favorite email trick for cleaning up inbox clutter - automatically
articles.summary: A built-in email feature can automatically clean up inbox clutter from ads, newsletters, and social media updates, helping manage overflowing inboxes efficiently.

articles.title: After using Lenovo's new Yoga laptop, I'm wondering if Windows makers are running out of ideas
articles.summary: Lenovo's Yoga 7a 2-in-1 is versatile and well-built but features mid-tier hardware that makes it less competitive than rivals, raising questions about Windows makers' innovation.

articles.title: Beyond Vector Search: Building a Deterministic 3-Tiered Graph-RAG System
articles.summary: This article discusses building a deterministic 3-tiered graph-based RAG system that goes beyond traditional vector search methods. It focuses on improving retrieval accuracy through structured graph approaches.

articles.title: The Roadmap to Mastering Agentic AI Design Patterns
articles.summary: The article outlines essential design patterns for building effective agentic AI systems. It provides a roadmap for developers to understand and implement autonomous AI agents.

articles.title: A Hands-On Guide to Testing Agents with RAGAs and G-Eval
articles.summary: This guide demonstrates how to test AI agents using RAGAs and G-Eval frameworks for evaluation. It provides practical hands-on approaches for assessing agent performance and quality.

articles.title: Your ReAct Agent Is Wasting 90% of Its Retries — Here’s How to Stop It
articles.summary: ReAct agents waste 90.8% of retries on hallucinated tool calls due to architectural flaws, not model errors. Three structural changes can eliminate wasted retries entirely.

articles.title: Advanced RAG Retrieval: Cross-Encoders & Reranking
articles.summary: A practical guide to using cross-encoders and reranking techniques to improve RAG retrieval pipelines by implementing a second-pass ranking system.

articles.title: Why Every AI Coding Assistant Needs a Memory Layer
articles.summary: AI coding assistants require persistent memory layers to overcome LLM statelessness limitations and improve code quality by maintaining context across multiple sessions.

articles.title: National Robotics Week — Latest Physical AI Research, Breakthroughs and Resources
articles.summary: National Robotics Week highlights NVIDIA's breakthroughs bringing AI into the physical world. Advancements in robot learning, simulation, and foundation models accelerate real-world deployment across industries.

articles.title: Strength and Destiny Collide: ‘Samson: A Tyndalston Story’ Arrives in the Cloud
articles.summary: Samson: A Tyndalston Story by Liquid Swords is now available on GeForce NOW, allowing users to stream the mythic narrative game on various devices through cloud gaming.

articles.title: From RTX to Spark: NVIDIA Accelerates Gemma 4 for Local Agentic AI
articles.summary: Google and NVIDIA optimized Gemma 4 models for local AI execution across devices from RTX PCs to data centers. These small, efficient open models enable on-device agentic AI with real-time context processing.

articles.title: Understanding Amazon Bedrock model lifecycle
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock regularly releases new foundation model versions with improved capabilities and safety. Understanding the model lifecycle helps developers effectively plan and manage AI applications before migrating to newer versions.

articles.title: The future of managing agents at scale: AWS Agent Registry now in preview
articles.summary: AWS Agent Registry, now in preview via Amazon Bedrock AgentCore, enables enterprises to discover, share, and reuse AI agents and tools across their organization, addressing visibility, governance, and duplication challenges at scale.

articles.title: Embed a live AI browser agent in your React app with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore introduces BrowserLiveView component for React apps, enabling real-time visibility into AI agent browsing activities. This enhances user trust and control through live video feeds embedded with minimal code.

articles.title: MiniMax M2.7 Advances Scalable Agentic Workflows on NVIDIA Platforms for Complex AI Applications
articles.summary: MiniMax M2.7 enhances the M2.5 model for agentic workflows and complex AI applications across reasoning, ML research, software engineering, and office work on NVIDIA platforms.

articles.title: Running Large-Scale GPU Workloads on Kubernetes with Slurm
articles.summary: Slurm, an open-source cluster management system used by 65% of TOP500 systems, enables efficient GPU workload scheduling on Kubernetes. Organizations leverage their existing Slurm investments for large-scale AI training workflows.

articles.title: Cut Checkpoint Costs with About 30 Lines of Python and NVIDIA nvCOMP
articles.summary: NVIDIA's nvCOMP reduces LLM checkpoint storage costs using just 30 lines of Python by compressing massive model snapshots like 782GB checkpoints for 70B models.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study examines how generational differences influence consumer attitudes toward social media ads across various platforms, conducted in collaboration with CrowdDNA.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta aims for net zero emissions by 2030, developing carbon footprint mitigation technology and making it publicly available.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with unconventional background experience successfully developed innovative extended reality technology, demonstrating how diverse perspectives drive breakthrough advancements in emerging tech fields.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers develop methods to identify interactions at scale in Large Language Models, improving interpretability through feature attribution analysis to make AI decision-making more transparent and trustworthy.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Researchers develop information-driven methods to design imaging systems that optimize how well noisy measurements distinguish objects. The approach uses encoders and noise models to improve imaging system performance.

articles.title: RL without TD learning
articles.summary: A new RL algorithm uses divide-and-conquer approach instead of temporal difference learning, avoiding TD scalability challenges and performing better on long-horizon tasks.
